title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a beach clean-up.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you helping with the beach clean-up on Sunday? || Man: Yes, but I need to borrow some gloves. They provide bags, and there'll be people showing us where to go. [8]I was worried about getting there, but my neighbour offered me a lift, so that problem is solved[/8]. || Woman: Great. I'll see you there at ten. || Woman: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you helping with the beach clean-up on Sunday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but I need to borrow some gloves. They provide bags, and there'll be people showing us where to go. I was worried about getting there, but my neighbour offered me a lift, so that problem is solved.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Great. I'll see you there at ten.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I might ask someone else as well, just to be sure, but at least I have a much better idea now.

questions.question: What problem has the man solved?
questions.explanation: He says he was worried about getting there, but his neighbour offered him a lift.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a TV programme.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you watch that wildlife programme? || Woman: Yes, but it wasn't what I expected. The pictures were beautiful, and the presenter knew a lot. [9]I just thought there would be more about how animals live, not so much about the presenter travelling between places[/9]. || Man: I prefer programmes that focus on the animals too. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you watch that wildlife programme?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but it wasn't what I expected. The pictures were beautiful, and the presenter knew a lot. I just thought there would be more about how animals live, not so much about the presenter travelling between places.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I prefer programmes that focus on the animals too.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out. It sounds as if you have thought about it carefully, which is always a sensible start.

questions.question: What disappointed the woman about the programme?

options.C: There was not enough about animals.

questions.explanation: She expected more about how animals live and less about the presenter's travel.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a running club.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you joining the running club? || Man: Yes. I was nervous because everyone looked very fit in the photos online. [10]But when I emailed them, they said beginners are welcome and there are slow groups every Tuesday[/10]. || Woman: That's perfect. It'll be easier if you can run with people at your level. || Woman: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you joining the running club?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. I was nervous because everyone looked very fit in the photos online. But when I emailed them, they said beginners are welcome and there are slow groups every Tuesday.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's perfect. It'll be easier if you can run with people at your level.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe next time it will be simpler. Things like this often seem more difficult before you know the full story.

questions.question: Why does the man feel better about joining the club?

options.C: The club meets near his house.

questions.explanation: He says beginners are welcome and there are slow groups every Tuesday.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking about an online order.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did your headphones arrive? || Man: Yes, but not the ones I ordered. The delivery was quick, and the price was good. [11]The problem is that they sent the black pair instead of the blue pair I chose[/11]. || Woman: That's annoying. At least you can probably send them back easily. || Woman: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later. || Woman: At least the main point is clear.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did your headphones arrive?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but not the ones I ordered. The delivery was quick, and the price was good. The problem is that they sent the black pair instead of the blue pair I chose.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's annoying. At least you can probably send them back easily.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. It is worth taking a little time over it, instead of rushing and regretting the choice later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: At least the main point is clear.

questions.question: What is wrong with the man's order?
questions.explanation: He says they sent the black pair instead of the blue pair he chose.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a language app.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is that language app helping with your Italian? || Woman: Yes, especially with vocabulary. The short exercises are easy to fit into the day. [12]The only weak point is the listening practice, because the speakers talk too slowly and don't sound natural[/12]. || Man: Real conversations are always harder than apps. || Woman: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning. || Woman: That is worth remembering next time too.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is that language app helping with your Italian?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, especially with vocabulary. The short exercises are easy to fit into the day. The only weak point is the listening practice, because the speakers talk too slowly and don't sound natural.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Real conversations are always harder than apps.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense. Sometimes the practical details are more important than they seem at the beginning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That is worth remembering next time too.

questions.question: What criticism does the woman make of the app?

options.B: It does not teach enough vocabulary.

questions.explanation: She says the listening speakers talk too slowly and do not sound natural.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a local market.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you go to the Saturday market? || Woman: Yes, and I'll go again. Some stalls were expensive, but there was a good choice. [13]I especially liked being able to talk to the people who made the food and ask what was in everything[/13]. || Man: That's useful if you're buying something new. || Woman: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do. || Woman: I suppose that is helpful to know.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you go to the Saturday market?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and I'll go again. Some stalls were expensive, but there was a good choice. I especially liked being able to talk to the people who made the food and ask what was in everything.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's useful if you're buying something new.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I can understand that. It is always useful when someone explains the situation clearly before you decide what to do.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suppose that is helpful to know.

questions.question: What did the woman like about the market?

options.A: meeting the people who made the products.
options.C: seeing a larger number of stalls than usual.

questions.explanation: She says she liked talking to the people who made the food and asking about it.
